titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bhatt Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Pravin Bhatt, Richard Richie, and William Landis

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:32

did.unittitle: Bhatt family papers

descriptivenote.p: The materials are in Gujarati and English.

did.abstract: The collection includes correspondence and other materials, primarily written by Nanabhai K. Bhatt to his son Pravin N. Bhatt. The materials document the political and social climate in India from the late 1940s through the early 1960s. In addition, collection materials provide background information on Pravin Bhatt's career as a scientist at Yale University and internationally.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by Pravin N. Bhatt during his lifetime for materials in the collection in which he has copyright interests, after which copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bhatt Family Papers (MS 1705). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Approximately 34 publications (books, pamphlets, and magazines) were removed from the Bhatt Family Papers and given to the Southeast Asia Collection curator in the Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Identification of materials in this collection for the purposes of this description was made by Pravin N. Bhatt in discussions with Richard Richie and William Landis over the course of several months in 2008-2009.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Pancholi Collection of Gandhi Letters (MS 1483). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Nanabhai K. Bhatt was an Indian educator and colleague of Mahatma Gandhi. Dr. Pravin Bhatt, his son, is a researcher at the Yale School of Medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes correspondence and other materials, primarily written by Nanabhai K. Bhatt to his son Pravin N. Bhatt. The materials document the political and social climate in India from the late 1940s through the early 1960s. In addition, collection materials provide background information on Pravin Bhatt's career as a scientist at Yale University and internationally.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by type of material.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, unless otherwise noted, is from Nanabhai K. Bhatt to his son Pravin N. Bhatt and family. It frequently includes information about general family matters, including celebrations, traditions, travel, and health issues.

did.unittitle: From Pancholi family to Pravin N. Bhatt
From Nanabhai K. Bhatt to Haribhai Pathak, father of Indu Bhatt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nanabhai Bhatt wrote these letters from prison following his arrest by the British authorities for pro-Indian activities.

did.unittitle: From Nanabhai K. Bhatt to his sons Pravin and Batuk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written while Nanabhai Bhatt was in prison following his arrest by the British authorities for his pro-Indian political activities. His family did not know where he was for a month following his arrest.

did.unittitle: From Nanabhai K. Bhatt to Haribhai Pathak and Indu Bhatt
From Sarabhai family to Pravin N. Bhatt
From Pandit Sukhalalji to Nanabhai K. Bhatt
From Nanabhai K. Bhatt to Indu Bhatt, his daughter-in-law
From Nanabhai K. Bhatt to Pravin and Indu Bhatt and others in Calcutta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pravin Bhatt was studying at the School of Tropical Medicine in Calcutta at the time, and became ill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter in English to Secretary of Health for the Indian government.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To Indu Bhatt and friends about going to the United States and to Pravin Bhatt about his wife joining him in the U.S.

did.unittitle: From Jivraj Mehta to Pravin Bhatt regarding job difficulties and from Nanabhai K. Bhatt to Pravin and Indu Bhatt regarding their return to the United States

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some letters written from Delhi, where Nanabhai Bhatt served as a member of the upper house of the Indian Parliament. Documents discussions with high-level government officials regarding potential jobs for Pravin Bhatt that might utilize his medical training in the U.S and possible living arrangements in Mumbai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters document Nanabhai Bhatt's work in Delhi and at the Lok Bharati (People's College), and his various travels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes details concerning a visit to the house of Swami Anand, a follower of Gandhi, near Bombay. Also includes information about attendance at UNESCO conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some correspondence relates to Pravin Bhatt's trip to Russia for research consultations.

did.unittitle: To Pravin N. Bhatt from Manubhai Pancholi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence concerns political and social topics of the day. Pancholi, a protege of Gandhi's, taught at Lok Bharati.

did.unittitle: To Pravin N. Bhatt, condolences on the death of his father, Nanabhai K. Bhatt
To Pravin N. Bhatt from Swami Anand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence on Bhatt's brother's sudden death while working in labor relations in an Indian textile factory.

did.unittitle: Pravin N. Bhatt, "Report of European Tour," typescript
Passports and passport photograph
Yale University Biological Safety Advisory Committee
Clippings and notes regarding Pravin Bhatt's time at Tulane University
Awards
Griffin Award (American Association of Laboratory Animal Science)
Slides and photographs of Lok Bharati, high school for Indian teenagers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nanabhai Bhatt was the founder of Lok Bharati (People's College).

did.unittitle: Slides of Gandhi, including the salt march

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides were given to Pravin Bhatt and were produced in circa 1970s.

did.unittitle: Mr. Pancholi's lecture at Yale on the donation of the Gandhi letters
Gandhi's salt march in 1928

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original compilation of film clips, in chronological order, documenting the salt march. Compiled by Mohan Parikh, not commercially produced.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives.

did.unittitle: Use copy
